senses.definition: ‌a board connected to a wall or a piece of furniture on which books are kept

examples.x: She organized her collection of novels neatly on the bookshelf in the living room.
The bookshelf in the study was filled with textbooks and academic journals.
They installed floating bookshelves in the children's room to encourage reading.

senses.definition: a hanging piece of cloth or other materials that covers a window, opening, etc.

examples.x: She drew the curtains to block out the sunlight streaming into the room.
He replaced the old curtains with new ones to freshen up the decor.
They installed curtains with thermal lining to help regulate room temperature.

senses.definition: a piece of soft thin paper that is disposable and is used for cleaning

examples.x: She reached for a tissue to wipe away her tears.
He sneezed into a tissue to prevent spreading germs.
They stocked up on tissues before the cold season to prepare for runny noses.
He used a tissue to blot the excess oil from his face.
She placed a tissue over the spill to absorb the liquid.

senses.definition: a square piece of cloth or paper we use to protect our clothes and clean our mouth or hands while eating

examples.x: He reached for a napkin to wipe his hands after enjoying the barbecue ribs.
She unfolded her napkin and placed it neatly on her lap before starting her meal.
The restaurant provided cloth napkins for a touch of elegance during the dining experience.
They used paper napkins at the picnic to clean up spills and messy fingers.
The waitress brought extra napkins when she noticed the children making a mess with their spaghetti.

senses.definition: a soft and thick substance we put on a toothbrush to clean our teeth

examples.x: She squeezed a small amount of toothpaste onto her toothbrush before brushing her teeth.
The toothpaste spilled onto the sink, creating a sticky mess.
The dentist recommended a whitening toothpaste for brighter teeth.
Using fluoride toothpaste helps prevent cavities.
She ran out of toothpaste and made a note to buy more at the store.

senses.definition: a sharp-edged tool used for shaving hair off the body or face

examples.x: She replaced the blades in her disposable razor for a smooth shave.
They stocked up on razors and shaving cream at the store.
He nicked himself with the razor while shaving and applied a small bandage.

senses.definition: a rounded glass that is inside an electric lamp and from which light shines

examples.x: He replaced the burnt-out light bulb in the ceiling fixture with a new one.
She unscrewed the light bulb from the lamp and shook it gently to check for damage.
The energy-efficient LED light bulbs lasted much longer than traditional incandescent ones.
They installed smart light bulbs that could be controlled remotely via a smartphone app.
He accidentally broke the light bulb while changing it and had to sweep up the shards carefully.

senses.definition: something such as a button or key that turns a machine, lamp, etc. on or off

examples.x: She pressed the switch to turn on the light in the room.
He flipped the switch to activate the power to the machinery.
He accidentally bumped into the switch, turning off the power to the computer.
The switch on the blender had multiple settings for different blending speeds.

senses.definition: a place where we can plug in electric devices to connect them to the electricity

examples.x: He plugged his phone charger into the outlet to charge his phone.
The power strip had multiple outlets for plugging in various electronic devices.
She searched for an outlet to plug in her laptop at the airport.
The kitchen island had built-in outlets for convenient access while cooking.
They installed outdoor outlets in the backyard for powering lights and tools.

senses.definition: a tool used to cut paper, cloth, etc. with two handles and two sharp edges, joined in the middle

examples.x: He carefully trimmed the fabric with sharp scissors to create a clean edge.
The tailor used scissors to snip loose threads and adjust garment lengths.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment that flows water all over your body from above
the seat we use for getting rid of bodily waste

examples.x: The toilet in the modern bathroom featured sleek design and water-efficient technology.
A clogged toilet led to a humorous yet embarrassing situation during the family gathering.
The bathroom remodel included a state-of-the-art toilet with built-in bidet functions for added comfort.
The children learned the importance of proper toilet etiquette during their potty training phase.

senses.definition: a large and open container that has a water supply and you can use to wash your hands, dishes, etc. in

examples.x: She filled the sink with warm, soapy water to wash the dirty dishes.
He rinsed his hands under the running water in the sink after gardening.
The bathroom sink had a leak, so they called a plumber to fix it.
They installed a deep farmhouse sink in the kitchen for washing large pots and pans.
The utility sink in the laundry room was perfect for soaking stained clothing.

senses.definition: a large container that we fill with water and sit or lie in to wash our body

examples.x: She soaked in the bathtub with bubbles and candles for a relaxing evening.
He installed a clawfoot bathtub in the bathroom for a vintage touch.
The whirlpool bathtub had jets that provided a soothing massage while soaking.
They renovated the bathroom and replaced the old bathtub with a modern, freestanding design.
She enjoyed a long soak in the bathtub after a strenuous workout at the gym.

senses.definition: a piece of cloth or paper that you use for drying your body or things such as dishes

examples.x: He used a towel to dry his hair after swimming.
I dried my hands with a soft towel.
I usually use a microfiber towel for cleaning glass surfaces.
The hotel provides fresh towels for the guests every day.

senses.definition: a flat surface made of glass that people can see themselves in

examples.x: He adjusted his tie while gazing into the mirror above the dresser.
She looked at her reflection in the mirror before leaving the house.
The bathroom mirror was foggy from the steam of the hot shower.
She applied makeup in front of the magnifying mirror on the vanity.

senses.definition: a specially shaped piece of metal used for locking or unlocking a door, starting a car, etc.

examples.x: She reached into her purse to retrieve the key to the front door.
He turned the key in the ignition to start the car's engine.
The spare key was hidden under a rock near the front porch.
They needed to have the key duplicated for the new employee.
She inserted the key into the lock and turned it to open the door.

senses.definition: a device that firmly fastens a door, closet, etc. and usually needs a key to be opened

examples.x: He turned the key in the lock to secure the front door before leaving.
She clicked the lock shut on the suitcase before boarding the plane.
He fiddled with the lock on the gate until it finally opened.
The safe had a sturdy lock to protect valuables stored inside.

senses.definition: an open pipe that is attached beneath the edge of a building roof and carries rainwater away

examples.x: The gutter along the roofline prevented rainwater from dripping onto the walkway below.
They cleaned out the leaves and debris clogging the gutter to prevent water damage.
The downspout carried water from the gutter down to the ground.
The gutter overflowed during heavy rainfall, causing water damage to the foundation.
She heard the sound of rainwater rushing through the gutter during the storm.

senses.definition: a bag made of cloth that is filled with soft material, used for leaning or sitting on

examples.x: She fluffed up the cushions on the sofa for added comfort.
He placed a decorative cushion on the armchair to complement the room's decor.
They added cushions to the outdoor furniture to make it more comfortable for lounging.
The window seat was adorned with plush cushions for cozy reading nook.
She leaned back against the cushion while watching TV.
